content.problemStatement:
You have an array A=[A1,A2,…,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​,…,AN​] of size NNN.

Perform **exactly** KKK operations of the type:

Pick index iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N) and,


If AiA_iAi​ is even, replace AiA_iAi​ by Ai2\frac{A_i}{2}2Ai​​
If AiA_iAi​ is odd, replace AiA_iAi​ by Ai+1A_i+1Ai​+1
Find the maximum possible sum of the array AAA after all KKK operations are complete. Note that you aren't allowed to skip operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of 222 lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers NNN and KKK, the size of the array AAA and the number of operations.
The next line contains NNN integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible final sum of the array AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
It can be shown that the answer doesn't exceed the limit of a signed 646464-bit integer

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 for the first and only operation to end up with [5,12][5, 12][5,12].
Test case 222: Choose i=3i = 3i=3 for the first operation and i=2i = 2i=2 for the second operation to end up with [1,3,6,8][1, 3, 6, 8][1,3,6,8].
